title: All Hilltop Dwellers must be evacuated or forced to be public hosts

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that hilltops and mountain summits should be treated as sacred public spaces rather than private property. He proposes laws to restrict hilltop housing, require existing hilltop residents to open their land to public access, and eventually relocate residents with compensation, citing wildlife habitat preservation, fire scarring from roads, and a Roman Republic anecdote about a consul who moved off the hill to avoid appearing kingly.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Nobody should be allowed to live privately on hilltops; they are sacred public spaces
Should be required to open their land to public access or eventually be relocated with compensation
Development encroaches on mountain lions and bears and should be curbed
Roads and development permanently scar natural land and should be minimized
The high points of a community should never be private — they should be public like a museum, not a private home
Would be great for biking but pay-to-access ruins it
Rates this a category-1 political idea — a seed to plant, not an urgent mandate
